query_normalized: quantifying the complexity of knot diagrams via crossing number and braid index mathematics
rationale: This paper directly addresses braid index, which is one of the two key complexity metrics in the user's research question, and operates in the same mathematical domain (knot theory). It satisfies acceptance criteria (a) and (b) by measuring the same variable (braid index) on the same domain (knots/knotted surfaces), even though it focuses on 2-knots in $\mathbb{R}^4$ rather than classical 1-knots.
